blocks.text: Check off each part as you find it. Everything on this list comes in your NanoXRP kit.
**Be gentle with electronics.** Hold boards by their edges, keep liquids away, and never force a connector — if it doesn't fit, check the orientation first.

blocks.alt: Motor wires and battery connected to the board

blocks.text: Connect the motor wires and the battery to the board.

blocks.alt: Wheels pressed onto motors; motors and casters in the flipped chassis

blocks.text: Press the wheels onto the motors — nub toward the motor, and **don't push on the encoder magnet**. Then press the motors and casters into the flipped chassis, with the encoder ports facing down.

blocks.alt: Wires routed through the rear wheel wells

blocks.text: Route the wires through the rear wheel wells.

blocks.alt: Bottom plate clipped on with motor wires plugged in

blocks.text: Clip on the bottom plate — check the line-follow sensor orientation — and plug the motor wires into the motors.

blocks.alt: Sonar in the chassis with the battery cover clipped snug against it

blocks.text: Connect the sonar — be gentle with the 4-pin Dupont connector — then slide it into the chassis and clip the battery cover snug against the sonar.

questions.prompt: When pressing a wheel onto a motor, which way does the nub face?
questions.feedbackCorrect: Right — nub toward the motor, and never push on the encoder magnet.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: Look back at Step 2: the nub faces toward the motor.

questions.prompt: What should you be extra careful with when connecting the sonar?
questions.feedbackCorrect: Exactly — the 4-pin connector bends easily, so be gentle.
questions.feedbackIncorrect: Check Step 5: the 4-pin Dupont connector is the delicate part.

blocks.text: Your NanoXRP is built! Next stop: *Learn* mode, where you'll make it drive.
